By employing some of these tactics, you will effectively be able to reshape your counterparts' perception of reality and undoubtedly improve your chances of achieving your goals. The word manipulation has a very negative connotation. We all think of a puppet master pulling strings and trying to get people to conform to his will. It is true that manipulating someone can be nefarious, but in the context of negotiations, we want you to conceptualize it as nothing more than guiding your counterpart. You're just adjusting their frame of reference so you can get what is fair for yourself. While decisions people make seem irrational, it doesn't mean that there aren't consistent patterns, principles, and rules behind how we act. Once you begin to understand these constants, you will start to see ways of using them to influence others and improve your outcomes.
